symptom: Azure subscription restricted from creating specific VM sizes in certain regions; quota request rejected by commerce/PG team even for large customers
rootCause: New subscriptions may have offer restrictions limiting available VM SKUs or core quotas in specific regions; quota is subscription-scoped and cannot be automatically shared between subscriptions
solution: Request to move unused quota cores from an older subscription (with available unused capacity) to the new restricted subscription. Submit quota move request specifying: old subscription ID, new subscription ID, VM size, and number of cores to transfer. Note: no official process guarantees success; set expectations with customer accordingly.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=====1. Process=======/1.4 [Offer Restriction] A possible workaround of O.md

symptom: Windows VM backup fails at snapshot operation with error ExtensionFailedTimeoutVMNetworkUnresponsive; backup extension log shows 'The underlying connection was closed: An unexpected error occurred on a receive' and 503 HTTP errors to Azure Storage
rootCause: TLS version mismatch: Azure Storage requires TLS 1.1 or 1.2, but Windows .NET applications default to TLS 1.0/SSL 3.0 (when DisableStrongCrypto is set), causing the secure channel establishment to fail
solution: Add SchUseStrongCrypto DWORD value=1 under all .NET Framework registry paths for both 32-bit and 64-bit: [HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727], [HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319], [HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727], [HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319]. PG is planning a long-term fix to use TLS 1.1/1.2 without registry tweak.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.1 [Backup][WinVM][Error Code -ExtensionFailedTi.md

symptom: Azure Files and Folders (MARS agent) backup job completes with warning: certain files could not be backed up; LastBackupFailedFiles list contains files from WindowsAzure diagnostics folder
rootCause: File locked in use by another process during backup: C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Diagnostics.IaaSDiagnostics is continuously updated by the VM Diagnostics extension and was locked at backup time
solution: If warning is about the VM diagnostics log folder (C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Diagnostics.IaaSDiagnostics), safely ignore the warning as it does not affect VM data. Alternatively, exclude this file/folder from backup scope via backup settings: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/backup-azure-manage-windows-server#manage-exclusion-settings
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.2 [Backup]Files and Folders backup job successf.md

symptom: Azure VM backup fails with error: 'Extension installation failed due to a COM+ error. Please restart windows service COM+ System Application (COMSysApp)'; or 'ProviderNotInCommonFolder StatusCode.FailedVCRedistNotInstalled' in VMSnapshot extension log
rootCause: Two causes: (1) IaaS VM provider service (required for backup) is missing from the VM; (2) VCRedist not installed on the VM - the VMSnapshot extension depends on a specific VCRedist version to register iaasvm_ps.dll via regsvr32
solution: Step 1: Check if IaaS VM provider service exists; if not, add required registry keys (per CSS support wiki) so next backup trigger auto-installs the service. Step 2: Download and install the required VCRedist package (https://my.visualstudio.com/Downloads?pid=1452). Step 3: If VCRedist installation itself fails, escalate to queue APAC Developer DSC with: (a) which VCRedist version is needed, (b) the installation error message.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.5 [backup] Azure VM backup issue with COM+ erro.md

symptom: Azure VM recovery points are not being deleted/cleaned even after their retention period has expired; old recovery points persist in Recovery Services Vault
rootCause: When backup is disabled with 'Retain Backup' option selected (sets IsUnprotectWithRetainData=1 in backend), Azure Backup GC intentionally bypasses garbage collection for all recovery points of that data source regardless of expiry date — this is by design
solution: This is expected behavior. Verify using Kusto: (1) query BCMBackupStats to check RecoveryPointId and IsExpired status; (2) query BMSProtectionStats to check IsUnprotectWithRetainData flag. Recovery points under 'Retain Backup' protection must be manually deleted via portal or PowerShell. No automatic cleanup will occur.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.6 [backup] recovery points won't be cleaned if.md

symptom: Azure VM backup snapshot unexpectedly deleted without user action; customer receives email alert about snapshot deletion that they did not initiate
rootCause: Subscription was upgraded from VM Backup Stack V1 to V2. In V2, snapshots are retained locally for 7 days and then automatically expire and get deleted — this behavior does not exist in V1. Upgrade applies at subscription level affecting all VMs in that subscription.
solution: Check Kusto BCMBackupStats table for the subscription: query 'IsInstantRPEnabled' column (1=V2 stack, 0=V1). If V2, local snapshot auto-deletion after 7 days is expected behavior. Inform customer this is by design and snapshots can still be restored from vault. Query: BCMBackupStats | where PreciseTimeStamp > ago(7d) | where SubscriptionId == '<subID>' | project PreciseTimeStamp, StartTime, VMName, TaskId, IsInstantRPEnabled
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.4 [backup]how to check if the azure VM backup i.md

symptom: ASR Mobility service shows old version (e.g., 9.15.2) in Azure Portal after upgrade to newer version (e.g., 9.18.2) on source Linux VM; portal version does not update even after confirming upgrade log shows success
rootCause: Upgrade completed with exit code 209 (EP0903) meaning the upgrade succeeded but requires a system restart for some changes to take effect; portal still reflects the old version until the source VM is rebooted
solution: Reboot the source Linux/Windows VM to allow system changes from the mobility service upgrade to take effect. After reboot, the Azure Portal should reflect the correct updated version. Note: exit code 209 and error code EP0903 are normal indicators of a successful-but-pending-restart upgrade.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=====1. Process=======/1.8 [Process]PG engage process for ABRS.md

symptom: Kusto query returns masked/obfuscated PII data (e.g., clientIpAddress shows as {PII:Hxxx(<mixed_data>)}) instead of actual IP address or other sensitive values
rootCause: Kusto has compliance requirements that mask sensitive PII data (e.g., client IP addresses, user identifiers) in query results to protect privacy
solution: Use Geneva raw data via Jarvis to retrieve unmasked values. For ARM requests: find the source namespace in Jarvis — ARM records the unmasked clientIpAddress directly. For other Kusto databases with PII data, refer to MCVKB 7.10 ([7.10 [MCVKB][Jarvis] How to map jarvis]) for the Kusto-to-Jarvis namespace mapping.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 8. Kusto=======/8.10 How to get value for PII data in kusto.md

symptom: Need to quickly find VM ContainerID, NodeID, TenantName, RoleInstanceName for VMs under a subscription for troubleshooting (e.g., running HostAnalyzer requires ContainerID and NodeID at the exact incident time)
solution: Query LogContainerSnapshot table in azurecmmc Kusto cluster (requires RAMWeb Project 12894 access). Query: LogContainerSnapshot | where PreciseTimeStamp >= ago(1h) and subscriptionId == 'SubscriptionID' — Returns all VMs with ContainerID, NodeID, TenantName, RoleInstanceName. Adjust time range to the incident window to find historical ContainerID/NodeID when they may have changed. Also use Orb (search by SubID) for more detailed information including Container Snapshot, Role Instance, VMA event, watsup.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 8. Kusto=======/8.2 [Kusto] Query Tenant ID, Node ID etc informati.md

symptom: ASR VMware-to-Azure (V2A) replication stuck at 0% after unexpected power off on Configuration Server; disable and re-enable replication in portal shows no errors but replication remains at 0%
rootCause: Unexpected power off (and recovery) caused duplicate/stale host entries in the Configuration Server MySQL database (svsdb1 hosts table); the portal is misled by the duplicate host entries, and re-enabling replication registers against the duplicate data without clearing it
solution: 1. Find MySQL root password in amethyst.conf on Configuration Server. 2. Backup DB: mysqldump -u root -p<password> svsdb1 > C:Tempdbdump.sql. 3. Connect: mysql -u root -p<password> svsdb1. 4. Query duplicates: SELECT id as hostid, name, ipaddress, ostype, from_unixtime(lasthostupdatetime) as heartbeat FROM hosts WHERE name!="InMageProfiler". 5. For each stale/duplicate entry run in C:homesvsystems in: perl Unregister-ASRComponent.pl -IPAddress <IP> -Component Source. 6. Uninstall Mobility service on source VM and reboot. 7. Re-enable replication in Azure Portal.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.1 [ASR-V2A}Duplicated register entries in CS my.md

symptom: ASR V2A: VMware cloned Linux VM replication stuck at 0%; both original and cloned VM replication fail; cxps.err.log shows session conflicts and "can not be opened by multiple sessions" errors
rootCause: Cloned VM inherits the same AgentGeneratedId (host ID generated by Mobility Agent at install time) as the source VM; when both are registered to the same Configuration Server, the CS and Process Server encounter file locking conflicts and duplicate host entries in svsdb1 MySQL database
solution: 1. Disable replication for both VMs in portal (Disable replication and delete). 2. In CS MySQL, run query to verify host entries: select id,name,ipaddress,ostype,from_unixtime(lasthostupdatetime) from hosts where name!="InMageProfiler". 3. For stale/duplicate entries, run in C:\home\svsystems\bin: Unregister-ASRComponent.pl -IPAddress <IP> -Component Source. 4. Uninstall Mobility service from both VMs (Linux: /usr/local/ASR/uninstall.sh -Y). 5. Re-enable replication for both VMs.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.11 [ASR] VMware cloned VM may fail to enable re.md

symptom: ASR Configuration Server (deployed from OVF template) MySQL installation fails with HTTP 400 Bad Request; Onboarding-DRSetup.log shows "The remote server returned an error: (400) Bad Request" when downloading MySQL; packet capture shows TLS error
rootCause: Known bug in the OVF version: .NET Framework defaults to TLS 1.0/SSL 3.0 but the MySQL download server requires TLS 1.2; the connection fails during MySQL setup file download step
solution: Option 1 (Registry): Add SchUseStrongCrypto DWORD=1 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NetFramework\v1.0, v2.0, v4.0 AND HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NetFramework\v2.0, v4.0. Restart and retry. Option 2 (Silent install): Manually download MySQL 5.7 to C:\Temp\ASRSetup then run UnifiedSetup.exe with /q flag and MySQLCredsFilePath parameter. Option 3: Re-open Microsoft Azure Site Recovery Configuration Manager desktop shortcut and retry.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.12 [ASR] Unable to intall Mysql Error in config.md

symptom: ASR VMware-to-Azure: after protecting a UEFI-boot VM and failing it over to Azure, re-protect and failback to on-premises VMware fails with "Reprotect and failback is not supported for the VM because it has boot type UEFI"
rootCause: By design: ASR does not support failback to on-premises VMware for UEFI (GPT) boot VMs. During failover to Azure, ASR converts UEFI to legacy BIOS (MBR); but during re-protect, it still checks the original on-premises VM which is identified as UEFI/GPT and blocks the failback operation.
solution: Expected behavior per support matrix (docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/site-recovery/vmware-physical-azure-support-matrix). UEFI-boot VMs can be protected and failed over to Azure (converting to Gen1 BIOS) but cannot be failed back to on-premises VMware. To confirm UEFI: check diskpart for GPT/EFI partition, or query ASRKustoDB SRSDataEvent with keyword "UEFI". Customers who need failback must use BIOS-boot VMs on-premises.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.13 [ASR] VM with UEFI boot type cannot be faile.md

symptom: ASR V2A with proxy configured: initial replication stuck at 0% with error 90078 or 90079; portal shows Process Server to Storage Account connectivity in red; cbengine.exe cannot connect to Azure Storage Account; SDP log shows EndpointsConnectivityCheckFailed
rootCause: cbengine.exe runs as SYSTEM account and uses SYSTEM account IE proxy settings; the proxy settings configured for the regular admin account are not inherited; ASR/Storage URLs are not in the Trusted Sites for the SYSTEM account IE, causing cbengine.exe to fail connecting through proxy
solution: 1. Download PsExec (Sysinternals). 2. Run elevated: psexec -i -s "c:\Program Files\Internet Explorer\iexplore.exe" (opens IE as SYSTEM account). 3. Tools > Internet Options > Connections > LAN settings: verify proxy server and port are correct. 4. Tools > Security > Trusted Sites: add all ASR URLs (management.chinacloudapi.cn, graph.chinacloudapi.cn, login.chinacloudapi.cn, *.blob.core.windows.net). Verify cbengine.exe works normally and data transfers through proxy after fix.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.14 ASR _ V2A with proxy _ Initial replication s.md

symptom: ASR V2A UEFI VM failover: on version below 9.30 ASR creates a hydration VM (asr-tempxxx) to convert UEFI to BIOS during failover; on 9.30+ failover is blocked if target Azure VM size does not support Gen2
rootCause: ASR v9.30 (Nov 2019) introduced support for direct Gen2 (UEFI) failover without hydration VM conversion. But this requires selecting a Gen2-capable VM size in Compute & Network properties; if a Gen1-only size is selected for a UEFI source VM on v9.30+, failover is blocked with a Gen2 compatibility error.
solution: Upgrade ASR to v9.30 or above. Upgrade order: 1. Configuration Server (via Unified Setup). 2. Mobility Service on protected items (from portal). 3. Wait for new recovery point or run: "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Azure Site Recovery\agent\vacp -systemlevel -cc" for crash-consistent point. 4. In Compute & Network properties, select Gen2-capable VM size (see docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/virtual-machines/windows/generation-2). 5. Failover using new recovery point creates UEFI-based Gen2 Azure VM directly.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.15 [V2A] ASR version above 9.30 supports failov.md

symptom: ASR V2A enable replication fails with error 78006 / provider error 31337 "Protection could not be disabled on the virtual machine"; Linux VM running Docker with devicemapper storage driver; Kusto SRSOperationEvent shows EnableProtectionTask consistently failing
rootCause: Docker volume paths using devicemapper storage driver contain colons (e.g., docker-253:1-33832727-pool) which are valid Linux paths but invalid Windows path characters; ASR backend processing cannot handle the colon in the Docker volume path string
solution: Option 1: Switch Docker storage driver from devicemapper to overlay2 (recommended) or another supported driver without colon in path names. Option 2: If VM is stuck in partially-enabled state, use "Stop managing the virtual machine" in portal (PurgeVm) to clear the entry, then re-enable replication after resolving the Docker storage driver.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.16 [ASR][V2A]Enable Replication for VMware VM f.md

symptom: ASR Configuration Server shows disconnected in portal; CS logs show AADSTS700027 "Client assertion contains an invalid signature. Reason - The key used is expired" with specific certificate thumbprint and expiry date
rootCause: The certificate used by the ASR Configuration Server to authenticate with Azure AD has expired; the CS cannot obtain valid tokens and therefore disconnects from the ASR service
solution: 1. In Azure Portal, navigate to the CS and use Renew Certificate option. 2. Download new registration key. 3. On CS, run: %ProgramData%\ASR\home\svsystems\bin\cspsconfigtool.exe to re-register with new key. 4. Delete old expired certificates. 5. For Scale-out Process Server: run cdpcli.exe --registermt on the PS to update its certificate registration.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.18 [V2A] Multiple Issues.md

symptom: 需要通过 SAW 机器对 Mooncake VM 节点做控制台级访问（JIT + RDP/RDG 方式连接 Jump Box 再到 Host 节点）
solution: 前置条件：申请 Silo Membership（sasweb.microsoft.com, Org: CloudEnterprise）+ 物理智能卡 + CME 卡。正式步骤：1) SAW 登录公司账户；2) 在 Corp 机器创建/自认领 ICM（WATS team）；3) 申请 JIT Access (jitaccess.security.core.chinacloudapi.cn)，下载 RDP（Jump Box）和 RDG（Host）文件；4) 通过 Teams 将文件内容传到 SAW；⚠️ 不要直接打开 RDG 文件（如已打开，用 .rdg.old 备份）；5) 在 SAW 手动创建 RDP/RDG 文件，用 RDCM 连接节点；6) 选择 Container ID 连接 VM；7) 生成 Teams 会议邀请客户参与；全程需客户在线见证并录制。

symptom: LSI（Live Site Incident）发生后需向客户发送 RCA（根本原因分析）
solution: 使用双语（中英文）RCA 模板，包含字段：事件ID、标题、受影响服务、受影响区域、开始时间、恢复时间、事件总结、问题原因、下一步举措、隐私声明链接。隐私声明链接：https://www.azure.cn/zh-cn/support/legal/privacy-statement/。⚠️ 发送前检查该链接，Outlook 有时会把发件人邮件地址嵌入链接中。

symptom: 需要用 PowerShell 批量停止 VM 备份并删除 Recovery Services Vault（含 Soft Delete 已启用的场景）
solution: PowerShell 脚本（适用于仅有 VM 备份、无 SQL 等其他资源的 Vault）：1) Connect-AzAccount -Environment AzureChinaCloud；2) $vault = Get-AzRecoveryServicesVault -Name '<vault>'；3) Set-AzRecoveryServicesVaultContext -Vault $vault；4) Set-AzRecoveryServicesVaultProperty -VaultId $vault.Id -SoftDeleteFeatureState Disable（先禁用 Soft Delete）；5) $Containers = Get-AzRecoveryServicesBackupContainer -ContainerType AzureVM -Status Registered；6) foreach $Container: $Items = Get-AzRecoveryServicesBackupItem -Container $Container -WorkloadType AzureVM；Disable-AzRecoveryServicesBackupProtection -Item $Items -RemoveRecoveryPoints；7) Remove-AzRecoveryServicesVault -Vault $vault -Confirm。
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.10 Stop VM Backup & Delete Vault with Powershel.md

symptom: Linux VM 备份失败，错误 UserErrorGuestAgentStatusUnavailable / VMAgentStatusCommunicationError；waagent 日志在某时间点后停止；Kusto 显示 VMSnapshotLinux extension 状态 unknown；extension terminal state not reached
rootCause: waagent 脚本 shebang 为 #!/usr/bin/env python，但 Linux 系统默认 Python 已升级为 Python 3，导致 waagent 以 Python 3 运行出错（Python 2 API 不兼容）
solution: 1) 检查 waagent 脚本：cat /usr/sbin/waagent（查看第一行 shebang）；2) 修复 shebang：cat bin/waagent | sed 's_#!/usr/bin/env python_#!/usr/bin/env python2_' > /usr/sbin/waagent；3) 或重新安装 waagent（确保安装适配当前 Python 版本的版本）；4) Kusto 验证：查询 MABKustoProd.TraceLogMessage 按 TaskId 过滤，Level<=2 查 error/critical 消息。Kusto Endpoint (Mooncake): mabprodmcadx.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/MABKustoProd
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.11[VM]Python2和Python3.md

symptom: MARS system state backup fails with warning: Failed: Hr: = [0x80070057] BlbutilGetPartitionInformation:: volume is not GPT for SSBStaging.vhd under MARS Scratch folder
rootCause: SAN policy on Windows server set to offlineall, causing MARS to fail when accessing the dynamically created VHD (SSBStaging.vhd) used for system state backup staging
solution: Check and fix SAN policy via diskpart: 1) Run diskpart; 2) Run SAN to view current policy; 3) Run SAN POLICY=offlineShared (cluster servers) or SAN POLICY=onlineAll (standalone). Background: MARS creates SSBS (VHD where Windows Server Backup dumps data) and SSBV (dummy mount point for SSBS VHD) under Scratch folder each backup cycle. Wrong SAN policy prevents MARS from bringing the VHD online.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 13. Backup=======/13.9 [MCVKB][VM] Check SAN policy when using MARS.md

symptom: Event Viewer shows garbled descriptions or "The description for Event ID X cannot be found. The local computer may not have the necessary registry information or message DLL files to display the message"
rootCause: Event source component (message DLL / registry) not installed on the computer
solution: Install the corresponding Windows role/feature component: Windows 10 -> Settings > Apps > Optional Features; Windows Server -> Server Manager > Add Roles and Features. Internal tool Event Viewer Pro available at http://toolbox/evpro. If source already installed, try uninstall then reinstall.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.1 How to install Event Viewer sources.md

symptom: Windows Server 2012/2012 R2 VM ESU (Extended Security Update) installation fails; CBS log shows ESU: check failed E_FAIL (IMDS chain validation) or error 0x80090325 SEC_E_UNTRUSTED_ROOT
rootCause: VM missing intermediate CA certificates required by ESU IMDS validation chain. ESU licensing validates against Azure IMDS which requires specific DigiCert/Microsoft CA certificates trusted on the VM.
solution: 1) Check CBS log at C:\Windows\Logs\CBS\CBS.log; 2) Download required intermediate CA certificates from Azure Certificate Authority details page (https://docs.azure.cn/en-us/security/fundamentals/azure-ca-details); 3) Install via MMC: mmc.exe > File > Add/Remove Snap-ins > Certificates > Computer Account > Intermediate Certification Authorities > right-click > All Tasks > Import; 4) Retry ESU patch installation and reboot. Reference: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureIaaSVM/_wiki/wikis/AzureIaaSVM/712909/Common-ESU-Installation-Issue_IMDS
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.10 Windows 2012 ESU installation issue.md

symptom: VM deployed from custom Windows image fails with OSPTO (OS Provisioning Timeout) error; VM never completes OS provisioning after deployment
rootCause: Windows custom image not properly generalized: sysprep generalize not run, or VM powered on after sysprep completed, or pending Windows Update restarts existed before sysprep
solution: Troubleshooting: 1) Check C:\Windows\Panther\setupact.log for OS setup start (InstallWindows:Setup Phase = 4); 2) Check C:\Windows\System32\Sysprep\Panther\setupact.log for sysprep generalize (SYSPRP ParseCommands:Found supported command line option GENERALIZE) — OS setup records after sysprep timestamp = image not properly captured; 3) Also check UnattendGC\setupact.log and System event log Event 1074. Re-generalize guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/prepare-for-upload-vhd-image#generalize-a-vhd. Rules: (a) do NOT power VM on after sysprep; (b) no pending Windows Update restarts before sysprep.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.11 Windows custom image generalization check.md

symptom: Need to collect memory dump or process dump from Windows VM for crash / hang / high-CPU debugging
solution: Comprehensive dump collection reference. (1) Kernel/full crash dump: CrashDumpEnabled registry (0=None,1=Full,2=Kernel,3=Small,7=Auto) under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl; PowerShell script sets full dump + pagefile + NMI/CrashOnCtrlScroll trigger. (2) WER process dump: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\LocalDumps (DumpFolder/DumpType=2/DumpCount=10); correlate with Event ID 1000. (3) Task Manager: right-click process > Create Dump File. (4) ProcDump: procdump -ma <PID>. (5) VMware snapshot: vmss2core.exe -W (Win7/2008R2) or -W8 (Win8/2012+) using .vmss + .vmem. (6) Hyper-V: vm2dmp -bin .bin -vsv .bin -dmp .dmp. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/performance/memory-dump-file-options
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.12 Dump configuration.md

symptom: Windows 10 Gen1 VM fails in-place upgrade to Windows 11 with hardware requirement error: TPM 2.0 not detected, Secure Boot not enabled
rootCause: Azure Gen1 VMs have no virtual TPM or Secure Boot. Windows 11 upgrade checks TPM 2.0, Secure Boot, and CPU compatibility per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/whats-new/windows-11-requirements#virtual-machine-support
solution: Recommended: migrate to Gen2 VM (supports vTPM + Secure Boot). Short-term workaround (unsupported): 1) Download Windows 11 Installation Assistant; 2) Registry bypass: reg add HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PCHC /v UpgradeEligibility /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f; delete AppCompatFlags\CompatMarkers/Shared/TargetVersionUpgradeExperienceIndicators; add HwReqChkVars=SQ_SecureBootCapable=TRUE,SQ_SecureBootEnabled=TRUE,SQ_TpmVersion=2 under AppCompatFlags\HwReqChk; add AllowUpgradesWithUnsupportedTPMOrCPU=1 under HKLM\SYSTEM\Setup\MoSetup; 3) Run Installation Assistant.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.13 [Win] Win10 inplace upgrade to Win11 with uns.md

symptom: RDP to Azure Windows VM fails after May 2018 update: "An authentication error has occurred. The function requested is not supported. This could be due to CredSSP encryption oracle remediation"
rootCause: May 2018 cumulative update (CVE-2018-0886 fix) changed Group Policy Encryption Oracle Remediation default from Vulnerable to Mitigated. Patched client + unpatched server = RDP blocked.
solution: 3 options: (1) Use unpatched client / Console / Bastion to access VM, install May 2018 KB on VM (Win2008R2: KB4103712; Win2012R2: KB4103715), reboot; (2) Temporarily connect from unpatched client; (3) Non-production: set GP AllowEncryptionOracle=2 on client (HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\CredSSP\Parameters). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/windows/credssp-encryption-oracle-remediation
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.2 2018年5月累积更新导致Windows虚拟机远程连接问题的总结.md

symptom: Certificate thumbprint validation fails even though thumbprint appears visually correct after removing spaces; PowerShell or ARM deployment reports thumbprint invalid
rootCause: MMC Certificate Properties dialog embeds an invisible zero-width Unicode character at the beginning of the thumbprint value when copied to clipboard. After manually removing spaces, this hidden character remains.
solution: Do NOT copy certificate thumbprint from MMC UI. Use PowerShell: dir Cert:\CurrentUser\My or dir Cert:\LocalMachine\My — output has no hidden characters. If already copied from MMC, position cursor at very start of string and delete any character before the first visible hex digit.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.3 Be careful when using management console to ge.md

symptom: Windows Server VM fails to boot: winload.exe not found or BCD boot failure; VM stuck at boot error screen after disk corruption or migration
rootCause: BCD (Boot Configuration Database) entries have incorrect partition references. When OS disk attached to repair VM, System Reserved partition and OS partition get drive letters; BCD default entry may have wrong device/osdevice partition values.
solution: 1) Attach failed OS disk to troubleshooting VM; identify drive letters (System Reserved active partition = BCD location, e.g. H:; OS partition = I:); 2) bcdedit /store H:\boot\bcd /enum to view entries; 3) Fix {default}: set device partition=I:, osdevice partition=I:, integrityservices enable, recoveryenabled Off, bootstatuspolicy IgnoreAllFailures; also set {bootmgr} integrityservices enable; 4) Verify and detach disk; 5) Create new VM from fixed disk. Key: System Reserved is Active partition = BCD location; OS partition contains \Windows.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.4 [Win]A sample to fix windows no boot issue (wi.md

symptom: Windows Server 2008 R2 Azure VM fails to boot with error 0xc0000428 (Windows cannot verify the digital signature for this file) after installing KB4540688/KB4541500 (December 2019+ cumulative updates); affects subset of VMs
rootCause: bootmgr in System Reserved partition was NOT updated (still SHA-1 signed) while winload.exe was upgraded to SHA-2 via December 2019+ patches. Root cause: KB4474419 (SHA-2 code signing support, September 2019) was not installed as prerequisite before applying December 2019+ updates.
solution: Recovery: 1) Mount failed OS disk to helper VM (nested Hyper-V recommended); 2) Online disk, identify System Reserved partition drive (e.g. E:); 3) bcdedit /store E:\boot\bcd /set {default} nointegritychecks ON; 4) Offline disk and boot in nested Hyper-V (~30 min rollback); 5) Re-enable: bcdedit /set {default} nointegritychecks OFF. Prevention: Install KB4474419 (September 2019) FIRST, reboot, then apply December 2019+ patches. F8 does NOT work in 2008R2 Azure VMs.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.5 [Win] NOBOOT Issue(0xc0000428) after Installin.md

symptom: Azure custom VM image: timezone not set to China Standard Time; IPv6 not disabled; pagefile on D: temp disk; local admin profile not preserved after sysprep; custom unattend file not respected
rootCause: Azure does not support custom unattend files beyond AdditionalUnattendContent (only AutoLogon and FirstLoginCommands). Sysprep removes all user profiles. Moving pagefile requires reboot that cannot be done in simple PS commands alone.
solution: Use PowerShell DSC with RebootNodeIfNeeded=True: (1) Move pagefile+temp disk: MoveAzureTempDrive DSC module, set temp drive to Z:, re-enable pagefile on C:; (2) Set-TimeZone -Id "China Standard Time"; (3) Disable IPv6: Disable-NetAdapterBinding + HKLM Tcpip6 DisabledComponents=0xff; (4) Profile preserve: use CopyProfile=true in unattend - but Azure-provisioned users will have NO password and must reset via portal. Azure only supports AutoLogon and FirstLoginCommands in AdditionalUnattendContent.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.6 Key learnings in customizing VM images in Azur.md

symptom: Windows Azure VM experiencing low memory, OOM (out-of-memory), high paging, application failures or slow performance due to memory exhaustion or memory leak
rootCause: Either (1) insufficient physical RAM causing excessive paging to disk; or (2) memory leak - application fails to release memory (Private Bytes / Committed Bytes grow indefinitely). SQL Server without max_server_memory can allocate all RAM.
solution: (1) PerfInsights: run General VM Slow analysis - check Findings + Top Memory Consumers + High Memory Usage Periods. Alert thresholds: %Committed Bytes In Use >80% = pagefile too small; Available Bytes <5% RAM = investigate; <1% = definite problem; Pool Nonpaged Bytes >80% = Event 2019 risk. (2) Perfmon: logman collect Memory/* Process(*) Paging File(*) counters at 5s interval. (3) Process Explorer: monitor Handle Count + Private Bytes + Threads. (4) For leaks: process dump via Task Manager/ProcDump or OS dump via CrashDumpEnabled registry. (5) SQL Server: set max_server_memory = total RAM - 1~4GB (OS) - worker thread stack. Engage Windows EE GES for dump analysis.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/_4.7[Win] Low Memory or OOM Issues_Perf.md

symptom: Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) fails to enable on Windows VM; AzureDiskEncryption extension error code 2147942487; BitLocker extension log shows Win32EncryptableVolumeWrap::Encrypt failed with 2147942487
rootCause: Extra Group Policy registry keys under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE conflict with ADE required XTS_AES_256 encryption method. Keys like EncryptionMethodWithXtsOs=XTS_AES_128 override defaults and block encryption.
solution: 1) Check logs: waagent C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\WaAppAgent; ADE extension C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Security.AzureDiskEncryption\<ver>\Bitlocker Extension; 2) Compare HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE with a working VM - remove extra custom keys not present in default; 3) If previous failed extension: az vm encryption disable --name <vm> --resource-group <rg> --volume-type all; then az vm extension delete -g <rg> --vm-name <vm> -n AzureDiskEncryption; 4) Retry ADE enablement.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.9 [Win] Azure Disk Encryption failed to enable w.md

symptom: Need to benchmark Azure VM disk performance (IOPS, throughput, latency) on Premium Storage to validate SLA or diagnose storage performance
solution: Use DiskSpd (recommended) or IOMeter. DiskSpd: (1) Throughput: diskspd.exe -c1024M -d300 -W5 -w100 -t8 -o64 -b256k -r -h -L E:\testfile.dat; (2) Rate-limited 100MBps: add -g12500; (3) IOPS: diskspd.exe -c1024M -d300 -W5 -w0 -t8 -o64 -b8k -r -h -L E:\testfile.dat. Key params: -b8k=max IOPS, -b256k=max MBps, -o=queue depth, -h=disable SW cache (required). PerfInsights Benchmarking scenario automates DiskSpd. IOMeter: Max Disk Size 20480000 sectors, Outstanding IOs=32, Access spec 16KiB.

symptom: SLES 11 SP4 VM cannot enable ASR A2A replication; mobility service install fails with curl error 60 Peer certificate cannot be authenticated; AzureRcmCli.log shows RegisterMachine error 20498
rootCause: Missing root CA certificates (VeriSign Class 3 G5, Baltimore CyberTrust Root, DigiCert Global Root CA) in /etc/ssl/certs on SLES 11 SP4; hash symlinks absent causing SSL validation failure on bearer token request.
solution: 1) Check /var/log/azure/Microsoft.Azure.RecoveryServices.SiteRecovery.LinuxSLES11SP4/<ver>/CommandExecution.log and /var/log/AzureRcmCli.log for curl error 60. 2) cd /etc/ssl/certs; check+wget missing certs: VeriSign_Class_3_Public_Primary_Certification_Authority_G5.pem, Baltimore_CyberTrust_Root.pem, DigiCert_Global_Root_CA.pem. 3) Run c_rehash to regenerate hash symlinks. 4) Create manual hash copies: VeriSign->b204d74a.0, Baltimore->653b494a.0, DigiCert->3513523f.0. 5) Disable replication+remove replicated item, then re-enable. SLES 11 SP4 workaround only.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======12. ASR=======/12.2 SLES 11 SP4 VM cannot enable ASR A2A replicat.md

symptom: ASR replication performance issue -- need to measure disk write churn rate, RPO lag, upload vs processing throughput per disk in Mooncake (GetDsHealth() unavailable)
rootCause: Write churn rate exceeds available bandwidth or storage IOPS, causing RPO violations. Mooncake ASR Kusto lacks GetDsHealth() function available in global Azure.
solution: 3-step query on mabprodmc (MABKustoProd DB): (1) Get DataSourceId: TelemetryPEToProvider where SubscriptionId|project DataSourceId,VmName,OsType,ProtectedDiskCount. (2) Churn per disk: HvrApplySyncSessionStats where DataSourceId|summarize LastRPO, SourceChurnRateMBps=sum(DiffBlobSizeKB/1024)/uploadDuration, ServiceProcessingRateMBps, AvgWriteSizeKB, AvgOverlapRate, AvgSourceIOPS, CompressionRatio, TotalDataSizeUploadedMB by VhdStorageAccountType+DiskId. (3) Hourly timechart: join IncomingDataMB+ProcessedDataMB by 1h bin|render timechart. Use to identify if Standard->Premium storage upgrade resolves RPO issue.

symptom: ASR Hyper-V to Azure replication fails with error ID 18090 / error code 80790033: 'Starting initial replication failed for virtual machine as number of partitions in the os disk exceeded the number of allowed partitions'
rootCause: Generation 2 VM (UEFI boot, GPT partition) has more data volumes than ASR supports. For Gen2 VMs, ASR only supports 1 or 2 data volumes on the OS disk. Customer had 3 volumes.
solution: Combine or reformat the volumes to reduce to 2 or fewer data volumes. Check Hyper-V to Azure support matrix at https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/site-recovery/hyper-v-azure-support-matrix#azure-vm-requirements for partition limits.

solution: Upgrade Configuration Server and Process Server to the latest version. Use https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/site-recovery/vmware-azure-manage-configuration-server#upgrade-the-configuration-server or download directly from http://aka.ms/unifiedinstaller_sha

symptom: Azure VM unexpected reboot caused by compute node fault; Kusto shows HardwareFault with DCM FaultCode 60048/63023/63019/65298 and disk manufacturer SKhynix (models HFS960G32FEH-7A10A, HFS960G32MED-3410A, HFS960GD0TEG-6410A) with FirmwareRevision 20035P00
rootCause: Specific SKhynix SSD model with firmware version 20035P00 has elevated failure rate causing node faults. Azure auto-recovery moves VM to healthy node, causing unexpected reboot. The affected expansion unit has higher disk failure rate tied to this firmware+model combination.
solution: TSG: (1) Verify fault codes 60048/63023/63019/65298, (2) Check disk manufacturer=SKhynix via VMA Kusto, (3) Check FirmwareRevision=20035P00 via AzureDCMDb. All conditions must match. Provide bilingual RCA template (included). Azure engineering working on firmware improvements and disk qualification process updates.

symptom: VMSS Resource Health shows false 'Remote disk disconnected' / 'Unavailable' alerts on AKS node pool instances, but all instances are running normally in portal
rootCause: AKS cluster auto scaling uses 'Force Delete' feature which immediately releases attached resources, bypassing graceful shutdown. During Force Delete operations, Resource Health notifications are emitted but can be safely ignored.
solution: These Resource Health alerts can be ignored when they coincide with AKS auto scaling events. Verify by checking EventDataFull in Geneva for Resource Health category with the subscription and VMSS resource to correlate with autoscaler timing.

symptom: VM disappears from Update Management (UM) schedule update deployment preview panel. VM not showing in UM even though it was previously managed. Hybrid worker ping stops in backend Kusto logs.
rootCause: The crond service on the Linux VM was disabled. The PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks.py script (called every 15 minutes via cron) is responsible for pulling configuration from Log Analytics and installing/starting nxOMSAutomationWorker. Without crond, the hybrid worker process cannot be started or maintained, even after omsagent reinstallation or host reboot.
solution: Enable and start crond: systemctl enable crond; systemctl start crond. Then run: sudo -u omsagent python /opt/microsoft/omsconfig/Scripts/PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks.py to immediately reinstall missing packages. Verify hybrid worker heartbeat resumes in Kusto JRDSEtwHybridWorkerPing table.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Case Study/[UM] VM disappear due to cron issue.md

symptom: VMs do not appear in Update Management 'schedule update deployment' preview list at subscription level. Dynamic deployment or ARG-based deployment shows no machines. IMDS (169.254.169.254) returns no output on affected VM.
rootCause: The Linux VM is configured with a proxy that intercepts traffic to IMDS endpoint 169.254.169.254. Azure Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) does not support proxy access, and when proxy intercepts the IMDS call, the VM cannot be discovered by Update Management's dynamic scoping.
solution: Add no_proxy=169.254.169.254 to the Linux environment proxy configuration to bypass proxy for IMDS endpoint. This ensures IMDS responses are returned correctly and the VM appears in Update Management's dynamic deployment preview.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Case Study/[UM]VM disappear due to proxy issue 21090300400014.md

symptom: SQL Server on Azure VM shows high IO but CPU/memory are low. Customer claims Azure VM has IO issues but actual root cause is application-level IO pattern. Need to prove SQL query is causing the high IO consumption.
rootCause: SQL Server queries generating excessive IOPS that exceed VM/disk throttling limits. Customers often blame Azure VM disk performance, but the actual issue is the SQL workload IO pattern (sync IO, high outstanding IO) exceeding the Premium disk or VM-level throughput limits.
solution: Use sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats to measure SQL Server IO before/after query execution: calculate IOPS as (total_io_after - total_io_before) / interval_seconds. Compare with Performance Monitor disk IOPS to prove SQL is the IO consumer. For MySQL, use PERCONA pt-diskstats. Key formulas: sync IO IOPS = 1000ms / disk_latency_ms (e.g., 500 IOPS at 2ms latency); outstanding IO needed = disk_IOPS_limit / single_IO_IOPS.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======DB=======/[DBVKB] The relationship between IOPS of on premis.md

symptom: After sysprep and capture of AVD/WVD session host VM, the new VM created from the captured image is visible but not added to the WVD Session Pool; users cannot login
rootCause: Sysprep removes WVD agent registration. The RD Infrastructure Agent needs to be reinstalled with a fresh registration token after creating a VM from a captured image.
solution: 1) Check RD services are running (RD Agent Loader, RD Configuration, RD Services, RD UserMode Port Redirector). 2) Check registry HKLM\Software\Microsoft\RDInfraAgent for registration status. 3) Generate new registration token: New-RdsRegistrationInfo -TenantName <name> -HostPoolName <pool> -ExpirationHours 48. 4) Download and reinstall WVD Agent with the new token. 5) Check C:\Program Files\Microsoft RDInfra\AgentInstall.txt for errors.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 1 - 25th Oct 2021/[Philip] 2103050050000863 - Custom Image Issue.md

symptom: Linux VM filesystem corruption (Buffer I/O errors on dm device) after attaching 6th standard data disk with read/write cache enabled
rootCause: Azure restricts VM disk cache to max 4 standard disks (by slot order). When more than 4 standard disks have cache enabled, the host silently disables cache on extra disks via detach/reattach, which disrupts I/O and can cause filesystem corruption.
solution: 1) Use premium data disks to avoid the 4-disk cache restriction. 2) If using standard disks, enable cache on at most 4 data disks. 3) For stripe sets / logical volumes, use identical disk configuration (all cached or all uncached). Check code: VmBaseContainer.cpp RUNTIME_VM_CACHED_DISKS_MAX.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.18 [VM] The restriction of allowing only 4 stand.md

symptom: OS Provisioning timeout (OSProvisioningTimedOut) on CentOS 7.x VM; cloud-init fails with 'module object has no attribute JSONDecodeError'; waagent waits for ovf-env.xml indefinitely
rootCause: Unicode characters in VM tags cause cloud-init to fail parsing IMDS JSON response. This is a cloud-init bug in Python 2.7 where json.decoder.JSONDecodeError does not exist (it was added in Python 3.5). Fixed by Canonical around July 2020, but old distros may not have the fix.
solution: Recreate the VM without tags containing unicode characters. Add tags with unicode characters only after VM creation completes successfully. For newer images, upgrade cloud-init to a version that includes the fix.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.19 [VM] OS Provisioning (Cloud-init) timeout.md

symptom: VM provisioning fails with OSProvisioningTimedOut after creating image with temporary disk (D: drive) disabled/offline and pagefile moved to C:
rootCause: Azure VM provisioning script requires the temporary disk (D: drive) to be available. During unattended setup, Azure sets pagefile to D:\pagefile.sys. If the temporary disk volume is deleted or offline in the image, the unattended script fails and provisioning times out.
solution: Do not disable or remove the temporary disk (D: drive) in the OS image before sysprep. If temporary disk must be disabled post-deployment, use Custom Script Extension (v1.8 in Mooncake) to run scripts that move the pagefile and offline the disk after provisioning completes.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.2 [VM] Underline requirement of Azure VM tempora.md

symptom: VMSS creation fails with 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity' when deploying Service Fabric with multiple VMSS resources, but standalone VMSS of same size succeeds
rootCause: The failed VMSS had an invalid/inaccessible DSMS certificate URL (SSLCertificate.pfx with trailing space) in its dsmsConfiguration. All clusters rejected the deployment due to the certificate issue, but the error message was misleading (showing capacity error instead of certificate error).
solution: Compare VMSS deployment templates between successful and failed resources. Verify all certificate URLs in dsmsConfiguration are valid, accessible, and have no trailing spaces. Fix the certificate URL and redeploy.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.20[VMSS] VMSS creation failure using Service Fab.md

symptom: Linux VM WireServer not responding: OSProvisioningTimeout, waagent logs show HTTP GET IOError timed out, WireServer endpoint 168.63.129.16 unreachable
rootCause: Two root causes: (1) WireServer platform-side issue affecting multiple VMs on the same VNet; (2) VM has primary and secondary IPs on eth0 with static OS routing rule causing traffic to originate from secondary IP, blocked by hidden platform NSG rules (WIRE_SERVER_REQUEST / VNET_BLOCK_WIRE_SERVER)
solution: RC1: Telnet 168.63.129.16:80 from multiple VMs to confirm platform issue, file IcM. RC2: Validate OS routing with ip route show, ensure WireServer route uses primary IP as source. Fix: ip route delete/add 168.63.129.16 via gateway dev eth0 proto dhcp src primary-ip.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.25[VM] Linux Wireserver not Responding_Deploy.md

symptom: Cannot delete Azure VM: error Resource Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces not found during VM deletion
rootCause: The VM model in CRP contains a reference to a network interface already deleted from NRP. CRP tries to resolve the NIC during deletion but NRP returns NotFound.
solution: Create a new network interface matching the exact name in VM network profile, then retry deletion. Use CRP Kusto ApiQosEvent_nonGet and ContextActivity tables with correlation ID to confirm.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.26[VM] Cannot delete network interface not found.md

symptom: PowerShell-created Azure VM does not have VM Guest Agent installed, causing VM backup to fail
rootCause: PowerShell script did not include -ProvisionVMAgent parameter in Set-AzureRmVMOperatingSystem cmdlet.
solution: Add -ProvisionVMAgent parameter: Set-AzureRmVMOperatingSystem -VM $VMConfig -Windows -ComputerName $vmName -Credential $cred -ProvisionVMAgent
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.3 PowerShell created VM does not have guest agen.md

symptom: VM creation fails due to offer restriction in China East 2 and China North 2 regions; new subscriptions have 0 default compute quota after March 2022
rootCause: Since March 2nd 2022, newly created subscriptions no longer have EA default 350-core compute quota for CE2/CN2. Old subscriptions may also fail if not whitelisted.
solution: Raise 21V Commerce case to request quota. Urgent: field team to BG PMM. RDQuota# submitted by 21V. Commerce hours: 09:00-18:00 weekdays. Ref: https://aka.ms/AzureCapacity
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.30 [VM] VM is not creating due to offer restrict.md

symptom: Windows with Container VM images deprecated after 2023-04-30; VMSS/Service Fabric clusters cannot scale out or upgrade images
rootCause: Docker Engine Enterprise support transferred from Microsoft to Mirantis. Windows Server with Containers images (2016/2019/20H2 variants) deprecated.
solution: Options: (1) Migrate SF apps to AKS; (2) Update VMSS model to non-container image + install containerd; (3) Switch to Mirantis Container Runtime. Update terraform/ARM scripts.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.34  impact and solutions for windows with contai.md

symptom: Waagent Not Ready on CentOS 8 VM with custom-built Python 3.11: ModuleNotFoundError, NameError distro not defined, Unexpected centos version 8
rootCause: Customer replaced system Python3 with custom-built Python 3.11, installed WALinuxAgent from GitHub source. Custom Python lacks distro module; CentOS 8 EOL causes RDMA handler mismatch.
solution: Change waagent systemd ExecStart to platform-python (/usr/bin/python3.6); systemctl daemon-reload && restart waagent; Always install from distro repo.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.38 Waagent Not Ready (custom-built python).md

symptom: Windows VM fails to boot after cumulative updates (KB5040434/KB5040562). Stuck at update screen after portal shutdown.
rootCause: Portal shutdown triggers Host Agent 10-min forced power-off. If updates take >10 min, VM is terminated mid-update leaving non-bootable state.
solution: Shutdown/restart from within guest OS, not Azure portal. Ref wiki: Windows Fails to Boot After Cumulative Updates.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.39 Windows Fails to Boot After Updates.md

symptom: Ubuntu 22.04 VM reports false Resource Health Unavailable alerts daily. VM running fine. Kernel version 6.x below 6.2.0-1017.
rootCause: Known Canonical kernel bug below 6.2.0-1017 causes false health heartbeat failures.
solution: Upgrade kernel to >= 6.2.0-1017. Requires reboot. Verify with uname -a. ADO Work Item 59536.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.40 Ubuntu 22.04 VM report health alert everyday.md

symptom: Azure China VM ForceDelete API not working as expected; delete takes excessively long. Host Agent StopContainer ResultCode 0xc4520018.
rootCause: Platform issue in Azure China: ForceDelete not functioning, Host Agent container stop hangs. ICM 666633117. Now fixed.
solution: Fixed by platform. Troubleshoot: CRP ApiQosEvent_nonGet for ForceDelete op, Fabric VmServiceContainerOperations for ResultCode 0xc4520018.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.42 Azure China VM unable to perform ForceDelete.md

symptom: Cannot log in to Windows VM; VMAccess extension fails to reset password
rootCause: Local admin password lost; VMAccess extension may not function on certain OS versions
solution: Use Run-Command (Invoke-AzureRmVMRunCommand) to execute PowerShell script that resets local admin. No restart required. Prereq: VM agent running.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/2.9 [VM] Reset local admin password with Run-Comma.md

symptom: Linux VM sporadic app failures; dmesg shows Out of memory: Kill process; OOM Killer terminating processes
rootCause: Memory pressure: swap not configured, NUMA/HugePages, app memory leak, or insufficient VM size
solution: 1) Configure swap file. 2) Resize VM if workload peak. 3) Check NUMA/HugePages. 4) Protect process: echo -17 > /proc/PID/oom_adj. 5) Disable overcommit: vm.overcommit_memory=2.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.12[Linux] OOM Killer_Perf.md

symptom: No console output after Starting Switch Root when repairing Linux VM via nested Hyper-V
rootCause: Hyper-V Gen2 VM has no COM port enabled; serial console output not displayed
solution: Enable COM1 in Hyper-V settings (pipe name = VM name). Access via hvc.exe serial VMNAME or PuTTY serial to named pipe.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.14 [MCVKB]How to access Linux serial console on.md

symptom: VMSS scale-out fails: OperationNotAllowed/QuotaExceededWithPortalLink
rootCause: Regional vCPU quota exceeded
solution: Submit quota increase at https://aka.ms/ProdportalCRP
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/_2.27[VMSS] Scaling Issues Workflow_VMSS.md

symptom: VMSS scale-out fails: SubnetIsFull
rootCause: Subnet exhausted all available IPs
solution: Expand subnet CIDR or migrate VMSS to larger subnet. Remove orphaned NICs.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/_2.27[VMSS] Scaling Issues Workflow_VMSS.md

symptom: VMSS scale-out fails: ComputerNamePrefixTooLongForScaleOut
rootCause: computerNamePrefix too long for OS naming limits
solution: Shorten computerNamePrefix in VMSS model
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/_2.27[VMSS] Scaling Issues Workflow_VMSS.md

symptom: VMSS scale-out fails: InboundNatPoolFrontendPortRangeSmallerThanRequestedPorts
rootCause: LB NAT pool port range too small for VMSS instance count
solution: Increase frontend port range of inbound NAT pool on load balancer
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======2. VM & VMSS=======/_2.27[VMSS] Scaling Issues Workflow_VMSS.md

symptom: Red Hat 9 custom image VM boots into emergency mode; pvs shows 'Devices file PVID last seen on /dev/sda3 not found'
rootCause: RHEL 9 enables system.devices file feature by default (LVM2 device cache). When VHD is uploaded to Azure, device paths change, causing LVM to fail to find expected devices.
solution: 1) Add use_devicesfile=0 in /etc/lvm/lvm.conf (within devices {} section) to disable LVM2 system.devices cache; 2) Enable serial console: systemctl enable serial-getty@ttyS0.service; 3) Use Gen2 VMs for UEFI boot images; 4) Follow generic Linux image prep guide if no distro-specific doc exists.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.15 [Linux] Red Hat 9 custom image known issue.md

symptom: Data disk mounted under /mnt fails to mount after unexpected reboot on cloud-init provisioned Linux VM (Rocky Linux); cloud-init changes fstab entry for temporary disk from /mnt/resource to /mnt
rootCause: For Linux VMs provisioned with cloud-init, the temporary disk is mounted at /mnt by default. Cloud-init cc_mounts module rewrites fstab on reboot, overriding custom mount point /mnt/resource back to /mnt, which conflicts with data disks mounted under /mnt.
solution: 1) Avoid mounting data disks under /mnt/ (reserved for temp disk); 2) If /mnt/ mounting is required: create /etc/cloud/cloud.cfg.d/00-resource.cfg to set ephemeral disk mount to /mnt/resource, add DefaultEnvironment CLOUD_CFG in /etc/systemd/system.conf, then deallocate/redeploy VM.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.16 [Linux]For Linux VMs provisioned using cloud-.md

symptom: SUSE Enterprise Linux 15 SP6 VM repos not registered automatically; /var/log/cloudregister shows 'No response from' all region servers
rootCause: VM cannot reach SUSE regional servers and SMT server (smt-azure.susecloud.net) on port 443. Often caused by NVA deep packet inspection breaking SUSE certificate pinning, or missing whitelist for SMT/region server IPs. ChinaNorth3 region may not be listed in SUSE public cloud info.
solution: 1) Whitelist SMT server IPs (from susepubliccloudinfo.suse.com/v1/microsoft/servers/smt.xml) and region server IPs (regionserver.xml); 2) Verify SSL connectivity with openssl s_client -connect <IP>:443; 3) Ensure NVA does not perform deep packet inspection on SUSE repo connections; 4) Try sc-repocheck.py script for auto-fix; 5) For ChinaNorth3, add all ChinaNorth IPs.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.17 SUSE Repository Registration Issue.md

symptom: NVIDIA GPU driver disappears periodically on NVads_A10_v5 VM; nvidia-smi fails with 'NVIDIA_SMI has failed because it couldn't communicate with the NVIDIA Driver'
rootCause: Ubuntu automatic kernel upgrade (via apt-daily-upgrade.timer) installs new kernel version but NVIDIA driver is only installed in the old kernel's /lib/modules/<version>/kernel/drivers/video/ directory. After reboot to new kernel, driver is missing.
solution: Disable Ubuntu automatic kernel upgrade timers: sudo systemctl stop/disable apt-daily.timer, apt-daily.service, apt-daily-upgrade.timer, apt-daily-upgrade.service. Then reinstall NVIDIA Grid driver for current kernel version.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.18 [Linux] - GPU driver missing in Nvads_A10_v5.md

symptom: VMs created from custom image with CIS security hardening stuck at 'creating' until provisioning timeout; cloud-init.log shows 'unknown filesystem type udf' when mounting /dev/sr0; waagent waiting for ovf-env.xml
rootCause: CIS security hardening rule 1.1.1.6 disables UDF filesystem driver. Azure requires UDF to mount the provisioning media (/dev/sr0) containing ovf-env.xml at first boot. Without UDF, cloud-init cannot read provisioning data.
solution: 1) If image already captured: enable UDF by commenting 'install udf /bin/true' in /etc/modprobe.d/CIS.conf, mount UDF, verify with lsmod, then re-deprovision and recapture; 2) If preparing image: skip CIS rule 1.1.1.6 in defaults/main.yml, capture image, then re-enable UDF blocking in new VMs after first boot.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.4 [Linux] CIS security hardening impacts VM crea.md

symptom: Linux VM SSH authentication succeeds but session immediately disconnects; /var/log/secure shows pam_limits errors; console and single-user mode also fail to login
rootCause: nofile hard limit in /etc/security/limits.conf set higher than /proc/sys/fs/nr_open. pam_limits.so fails to apply the invalid limit, causing all login sessions to be rejected immediately after authentication.
solution: 1) Attach OS disk as data disk to rescue VM; 2) Either: change UsePAM yes to UsePAM no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, or remove pam_limits.so from /etc/pam.d/login; 3) Fix limits.conf: ensure nofile hard limit does not exceed /proc/sys/fs/nr_open value.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.6[VM] Linux VM cannot be logged in after modifyi.md

symptom: PAYG SUSE SLES VM needs registration or re-registration against Azure Public Cloud Update Infrastructure; SUSEConnect -s shows unregistered status
rootCause: SUSE registration configuration (/etc/SUSEConnect, zypp repos) is corrupt, stale, or was never initialized properly for Azure PAYG instances
solution: Run: rm /etc/SUSEConnect && rm -f /etc/zypp/{repos,services,credentials}.d/* && rm -f /usr/lib/zypp/plugins/services/* && sed -i '/^# Added by SMT reg/,+1d' /etc/hosts && /usr/sbin/registercloudguest --force-new. Verify with SUSEConnect -s. Backend workflow: VM connects to region server (regionserverclnt.cfg) -> gets update server list -> registers against first responding server -> IP added to /etc/hosts as smt-azure.susecloud.net
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======3. Linux=======/3.8[VM] PAYG SUSE SLES registration.md

symptom: Azure VM with P60 Premium disks managed by Oracle ASM shows Owait of 20% on the guest; severe I/O throttling observed; abnormal disk latency; Root VP Cores pegged at 100%; FE Errors reported in host metrics
rootCause: I/O throttling at VM level due to exceeding the VM-level IOPS/throughput cap when using multiple P60 premium disks; 2 Root VP Cores saturated at 100% causing FE Errors and additional latency
solution: Verify VM size supports the aggregate IOPS of all attached disks; resize VM to a size with higher uncached disk throughput limits; consider using disk striping instead of Oracle ASM direct attach to better distribute I/O
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.14 [MCVKB] Owait of 20% on the guest.md

symptom: Windows device registration (Azure AD Join) fails; WinHTTP callback error 0x80072efd (ERROR_WINHTTP_CANNOT_CONNECT) when connecting to EnterpriseRegistration.windows.net; ETL trace shows API_SEND_REQUEST failed
rootCause: Customer blocked the endpoint enterpriseregistration.windows.net at network/firewall level, causing WinHTTP connection failure during device registration discovery
solution: Unblock the endpoint enterpriseregistration.windows.net on the network/firewall; use Insight Client tool to convert ETL traces for analysis: install from cesdiagtools, configure symbol server, convert ETL to TXT for readable logs
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======7. Tools========/7.8 [VM] Leverage insight client to convert ETL tr.md

symptom: Linux Custom Script Extension (CSE) stays on Transitioning status indefinitely; operations like disk update fail because extension is stuck; Jarvis logs show VM extension failure; newly created VMs also show Transitioning
rootCause: Customer included a Reboot command in the custom script, causing the extension to never report success status back to the platform
solution: Remove Reboot command from the custom script; check CSE logs at /var/lib/waagent/custom-script/download/0/ (stdout/stderr), /var/log/azure/custom-script/handler.log, and /var/log/waagent.log
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.1 [Extension]Linux custom Script extension TSG.md

symptom: Cannot reset Linux VM password via Azure portal; VMAccess extension password reset fails; all user passwords lost on ARM Linux VM
rootCause: /etc/shadow file has immutable attribute (+i) set via chattr, preventing any modification including password reset by VMAccess extension
solution: Use Custom Script Extension to remove immutable attribute and reset password: create a script with chattr -i /etc/shadow and echo newpassword | passwd --stdin useraccount; deploy via az vm extension set with publisher Microsoft.Azure.Extensions; CSE runs as root
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.2 [Extension]leverage Azure VM Extension when yo.md

symptom: Set-AzVMCustomScriptExtension fails on Linux VM with error: binCustomScriptHandler.exe command not found; exit code 127; extension handler reports terminal failure for Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension
rootCause: Set-AzVMCustomScriptExtension installs from publisher Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension which is only supported for Windows; Linux requires publisher Microsoft.Azure.Extensions
solution: Use Set-AzVMExtension instead with correct publisher: Set-AzVMExtension -Publisher Microsoft.Azure.Extensions -Type CustomScript -TypeHandlerVersion 2.1 with -Settings and -ProtectedSettings parameters
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.3 [Extension] CSE Errors_AGEX.md

symptom: Linux Custom Script Extension (CSE) stuck in Transitioning state with status Enable in progress; CSE handler.log shows this script configuration is already processed will not run again
rootCause: WALinuxAgent was upgraded while CSE was executing; agent restart causes CSE to lose its execution state; on re-enable CSE detects same sequence number and refuses to re-run
solution: Wait for WALinuxAgent upgrade to complete then re-trigger CSE with updated settings (increment sequence number); check waagent.log for agent upgrade events; check CSE handler.log for already processed message; ref: github.com/Azure/WALinuxAgent/issues/749
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.3 [Extension] CSE Errors_AGEX.md

symptom: VM extension installation fails with error: Current sequence number, 0, is not greater than the sequence number of the most recently executed configuration, 0. ErrorCode: VMExtensionProvisioningError
rootCause: VM Extensions use sequence numbers to detect config changes. If re-run with same settings, sequence number does not increment and handler exits.
solution: Modify settings so handler assigns new sequence number, or use PowerShell -ForceRerun (get-date).ticks
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.4 [Extension] Current Sequence Number 0_AGEX.md

symptom: Extension installation fails with error code 1007: the system cannot find the file specified.
rootCause: COMSPEC environment variable changed from cmd.exe to another executable (e.g. Java/JDK). Extensions use .CMD files requiring cmd.exe.
solution: Reset COMSPEC to c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe. Reboot VM.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.5 [Extension] Extensions Known Issues_AGEX.md

symptom: Extension fails: The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure. Could not establish trust relationship for SSL/TLS.
rootCause: Missing Baltimore CyberTrust Root cert, or cert chain broken by 3rd party SSL inspection (Zscaler).
solution: Install Baltimore CyberTrust Root cert from cacert.omniroot.com/bc2025.crt and restart VM. Or configure SSL inspection to bypass Azure endpoints.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.5 [Extension] Extensions Known Issues_AGEX.md

symptom: Custom Script Extension fails with exit code 127 or error: The specified executable is not a valid application for this OS platform.
rootCause: Wrong extension publisher for OS. Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript is Linux only. Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension is Windows only.
solution: Windows: Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension. Linux: Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.5 [Extension] Extensions Known Issues_AGEX.md

symptom: Linux VM extension fails with non-zero exit code 126. All extensions fail to execute.
rootCause: /var partition mounted with noexec flag in /etc/fstab, blocking script execution where extensions run.
solution: Check /etc/fstab for noexec on /var. Remount: mount -o remount,exec /var
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.5 [Extension] Extensions Known Issues_AGEX.md

symptom: VMAccessForLinux fails on specialized VHD: /var/lib/waagent/ovf-env.xml No such file or directory.
rootCause: VM from specialized VHD without provisioning. ovf-env.xml generated during initial provisioning is missing.
solution: Recreate VM with proper provisioning, or copy ovf-env.xml from another VM, or upgrade VMAccessForLinux >= 1.5.6.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======9. Extension======/9.5 [Extension] Extensions Known Issues_AGEX.md

symptom: Cannot login to Azure VM, password incorrect, VMAccess extension not installed or broken.
rootCause: VM created without Azure VM Extension (VMAccess) or extension is broken/corrupted.
solution: Windows: reset password without agent via offline method. Linux: VMAccessForLinux TSG. Use rescue VM if agent unavailable.

symptom: WindowsAzureGuestAgent service not installed from MSI. TransparentInstaller.log: Create service failed with exit code 1639.
rootCause: Bug in sc.exe create command syntax in WinGA MSI v2.7.41491.1024. Missing spaces after equals signs.

symptom: Guest Agent: Unable to connect to remote server. SocketException: target machine actively refused it 127.0.0.1:8888.

symptom: VMs experience unexpected reboots during Live Migration Defrag in Mooncake.
rootCause: Live Migration Defrag enabled on clusters for capacity. Some VMs (<1%) may reboot.
solution: Kusto: LiveMigrationSessionCompleteLog | where PreciseTimeStamp >= ago(2d) and Tenant contains '<Cluster>' and triggerType contains 'defrag'. Report to cluster ops.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/========Planned Maintenance========/Live Migration Defrag in Mooncake.md

symptom: VM storage IO slow, application performance degraded (e.g. SQL Server), high latency on all disks
rootCause: VM-level throttling causes dependent blocking: one disk excessive IO triggers VM-level IOPS/throughput cap, blocking all other disks on the same VM
solution: Prevent VM level throttling: ensure total cached disk throughput < VM cached max limit; disable disk cache on mission-critical disks when temp disk has heavy IO; enlarge VM size if needed
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/_6.1 [Storage][Perf][Azure VM Storage Performance.md

symptom: Single-thread sync IO performance poor on Premium disk (P30), adding more disks does not improve performance
rootCause: Sync IO performance limited by disk latency (~2ms) not IOPS cap. Single outstanding IO gives only ~500 IOPS. More disks cannot improve single-thread sync IO
solution: Use async IO or multi-threaded IO to increase outstanding IO count. Need 10 outstanding IOs to saturate P30 5000 IOPS. Optimize application to use multiple threads
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/_6.1 [Storage][Perf][Azure VM Storage Performance.md

symptom: VM hang / unavailable due to high CPU, no platform restart or service healing triggered
rootCause: High CPU usage within guest OS causes VM hang. Azure platform service healing only handles platform-level issues, not guest OS level CPU exhaustion
solution: Monitor CPU from within OS; check ASC Resource Explorer > VM health tab > VMM availability impacts; use VM performance dashboard; set up guest-level CPU alerts
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/=====Members schedule======/Agnes Li/Case Study.md

symptom: Guest Agent shows Not Ready status, extensions fail to execute
rootCause: High CPU/memory within guest OS causes Guest Agent to hang and not respond. GA requires OS resources to run properly
solution: Check VM performance metrics during issue timeframe; use IID and WinGuestAnalyzer; review WaAppAgent.log and TransparentInstaller.log (Win) or /var/log/waagent.log (Linux)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/=====Members schedule======/Agnes Li/Case Study.md

symptom: VM unexpected reboot with Event 17 (E17) in RDOS
rootCause: VHD access failure: page blob requests to storage tenant fail continuously for 3 minutes, VhdDisk Driver triggers VM reboot to prevent data corruption
solution: Check RDOS for E17 events; investigate storage tenant connectivity and backend storage stamp health; also check for E21 (PaaS spillover disks) and E1 (Write Accelerator disks)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/=====Members schedule======/Agnes Li/VM readiness+lab.md

solution: 1) Upload custom RedHat VHD image following docs.azure.cn guide: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/virtual-machines/linux/redhat-create-upload-vhd. 2) Alternatively purchase RedHat subscription through 21V marketplace (世纪互联云市场) - contact yin.wei@oe.21vianet.com for details.
